title: Authentication, Passwords, Sessions, Cookies, and JOSE · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers authentication, passwords, sessions, cookies, and jose into a new credential verification and principal-construction contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of credential verification and principal-construction contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Apply modern password hashing, salt, work policy, input bounds, constant-behavior comparison, breach and reset controls, rehash migration, and secret separation.
Generate opaque session identifiers, store server-side state, rotate at authentication and privilege changes, expire idle and absolute lifetime, revoke, and prevent fixation.
Use jose verification with explicit algorithms, trusted keys, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, clock tolerance, token type, and bounded claim validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Password Verifier Policy
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 8787 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; reject a replay before any external effect; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Password Verifier Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply modern password hashing, salt, work policy, input bounds, constant-behavior comparison, breach and reset controls, rehash migration, and secret separation. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Password Verifier Policy, keep Password Verifier Policy connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Hashing a password once with a fast general-purpose hash is sufficient secure storage. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Password Verifier Policy.

hints: For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Authentication Principal Audit, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Authentication Principal Audit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Authentication Principal Audit, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 4835 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; reject a replay before any external effect; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the smallest principal after full credential validation and record redacted success, failure class, rotation, revocation, and recovery evidence.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Authentication Principal Audit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: An authenticated principal is authorized for every operation its token can name.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Authentication Principal Audit
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Authentication Principal Audit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 3216 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; reject a replay before any external effect; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the smallest principal after full credential validation and record redacted success, failure class, rotation, revocation, and recovery evidence.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Authentication Principal Audit.
steps.why: Retrieving Authentication Principal Audit through the concrete credential verification and principal-construction contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.
